# Transcrição: 05-The Pixel

- **Fonte:** 05-The Pixel
- **Duração:** 03:44
- **Idioma:** en

## [00:00 - 03:01]

Alright, hey guys, let's talk about the pixel, especially the health of the pixel in respect to in relation to coaching funnels. So as some of you guys may know, I'm not going to go too much into the purpose of the pixel, but there's really three main reasons why we use the Facebook pixel and why it's incredibly important to make sure that we have that set up with our clients before we get any kind of work started. So really the pixel is aimed at helping us track the success or lack of success of our Facebook ad campaigns, track back to which specific ads or which specific targeting led to a lead or a customer ultimately. It's also enables us to optimize our activities to make sure that once we identify the creative that's working really well, we can shift our budgets towards that and to create custom audiences. So that is so that we can retarget people that have shown some kind of interest or engagement with our funnel or our offer. Now when you start working with clients and with Facebook ads and making sure that the pixel is installed, you're going to be needing an important tool that is pretty fundamental to being a Facebook marketer and that is the pixel tool helper. So you can basically go inside of Google and just Google pixel tool helper. It's a Chrome extension. So you must be using a Chrome browser. And basically what it looks like is something like this up here. And when you're on a website, it will show up with a little green icon if it picks up on a pixel and then it'll show you the pixels, the events that are on that as well. So this is kind of an invaluable tool to be using. Now one of the things to double check is to see if your funnel is tracking properly as well is to go into the pixel tab of your ad account and we're going to click on details. And you'll see down here, it gives you kind of an overview of all the fires that have taken place and then all the different custom events or standard events that are across your funnels and that are being triggered. Now bear in mind that when you look under this tab, this is really important. It's going to show you triggers and hits regardless if they came from Facebook ads or not. So for example, audit opt-in, which is a custom event we created shows 271 triggers meaning 271 people opted in to whatever this lead magnet is. Now this doesn't mean that this came from Facebook. This can come from Google AdWords. It can be organic traffic. It could come from a social media post we did. To see how many of these 271 specifically came from your ads, you would look under your

## [03:01 - 03:44]

ads manager reporting column. So you'd go back into ads manager and it would be in your reporting columns that you would see how many of those actually came from Facebook ads. So this is showing you all the triggers regardless if they came from Facebook or not. So this is how to just kind of double check the health of a pixel. If you're auditing an account, a client, you can also see what kind of events they've used throughout their funnel. This isn't going to show you custom conversions. You'd need to go under a separate tab for that to see if they have set up any custom conversions, what's happening there, what's the health of those. And this really helps you get a clean idea or clean picture of everything that's happening throughout their funnels.
